authoritative_sources.title: ANSI/ISA-88.00.01-2010 Preview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISA-88 is the recognized batch-control standard for batch processes, procedural control, recipe types, modes and states, exception handling, batch schedules, batch history, and batch reports used across chemical and materials manufacturing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: batch workflow model and lifecycle-state definition

authoritative_sources.title: ANSI/ISA-88.00.04-2006 Batch Production Records Preview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISA-88 Part 4 is the recognized standard for batch production records, control recipe records, event and exception recording, and electronic batch-history structure.

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.119 - Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's PSM standard is the primary U.S. rule for process safety information, operating procedures, training, mechanical integrity, management of change, incident investigation, and pre-startup review for covered chemical processes.

authoritative_sources.title: CPL 02-01-065 Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's enforcement manual is the authoritative public inspection reference for how operating procedures, MOC, incident, mechanical-integrity, and other PSM evidence are reviewed during enforcement.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR Part 68 - Chemical Accident Prevention Provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 40 CFR Part 68 is EPA's primary rule for regulated-substance thresholds, prevention-program elements, incident investigation, emergency response, and recordkeeping at covered chemical facilities.

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring, Recordkeeping, and Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's Title V guidance page is the official permit-workflow reference for monitoring, recordkeeping, and reporting obligations that often gate whether batch production may proceed or be closed without unresolved deviation risk.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit-aware batch readiness and closure checks

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR 70.6 - Permit content
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR permit-content rule defines monitoring, recordkeeping, reporting, sampling, and inspection-entry conditions that batch execution must treat as authoritative evidence requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: Hazardous Waste Compliance Monitoring
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's hazardous-waste compliance-monitoring page is the official public reference for inspection types, record review, sampling inspections, and incident-oriented evidence handling under RCRA.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 55001:2024
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 55001 is the recognized asset-management standard for asset decision-making, risk, operational planning and control, lifecycle management, and data or information discipline tied to production readiness.
used_for: data and information discipline for maintenance evidence

workflow_stages.name: request intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture facility, process cell, campaign, batch, jurisdiction, tenant policy, and source-of-truth context before allowing any lifecycle-state change.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: batch readiness and pre-execution review
workflow_stages.description: Verify approved control recipe, released materials, ready equipment, open work-order status, calibration posture, permit conditions, and startup prerequisites before moving work into ready or running state.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: procedural execution and in-process evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Track batch state, procedural-step or phase status, throughput, in-process samples, operator observations, and event logging while the batch is executing.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: exception, hold, and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: When deviations, sample failures, blocked transfers, material problems, or changed field conditions appear, preserve evidence, move into controlled exception handling, and route to the correct authority owner.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and closure review
workflow_stages.description: Close batch work only when batch-history posting, yield reconciliation, sample disposition, required signoffs, and permit-sensitive evidence are present and traceable.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

artifacts: production orders, campaigns, control recipes, batch tickets, and batch production records
MES or batch-execution histories, operator logs, historian events, and batch-history reports
released material, intermediate, and finished-lot status plus genealogy and inventory records
in-process sample results, LIMS records, deviations, rework requests, and lab disposition notes
permit conditions, monitoring logs, sampling records, inspection findings, incident logs, and environmental reporting artifacts
asset condition records, work orders, maintenance releases, calibration certificates, and return-to-service evidence
safety observations, field-condition notes, containment records, and emergency or contingency posture logs
execution status updates, production status summaries, exception notes, completion records, and escalation packages

allowed: classify batch work as in-scope, blocked, out-of-scope, ready, running, held, rework-pending, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
advance, hold, rework, or complete work only when the controlling records support that state change
escalate: missing or stale permit, monitoring, sampling, inspection, calibration, maintenance, or incident records
field conditions that materially change environmental or safety risk posture
requests requiring engineer-of-record, EHS, maintenance, regulatory-reporting, or other authority-side decisions
conflicting public sources, permit conditions, or tenant policies that materially change the safe answer
refuse: marking batch work complete without authoritative completion evidence
engineering, environmental, or safety signoff outside documented authority
requests to bypass interlocks, operating windows, permit conditions, or inspection blockers to protect throughput

domain_failure_modes: A batch is started or advanced without the current control recipe, startup prerequisites, or permit context because record retrieval was treated as optional.
Execution exceptions are closed informally without preserved sample, deviation, or event evidence, producing unauditable rework or completion claims.
Field observations, containment issues, or alarm and calibration problems are treated as minor noise instead of signals that materially change risk posture.
Stale maintenance, calibration, monitoring, or inspection records create false confidence that the equipment and permit posture are still acceptable.
The lane drifts into engineering, EHS, regulatory-reporting, or legal signoff instead of remaining an execution-status and evidence-management specialist.
Conflicting public source requirements and tenant policy are collapsed into a confident answer instead of escalated for human resolution.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact PSM, RMP, Title V, and waste-inspection applicability by facility and process cell
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define the governing frameworks, but whether a specific batch line is covered depends on tenant-supplied permit stack, regulated substances, quantities, and unit configuration.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific completion authority and product-release owner
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline establishes evidence expectations and authority boundaries, but the exact release owner, signoff workflow, and closure artifacts still depend on the tenant's SOPs and system design.

notes: Authoritative execution outputs depend on tenant-designated ERP, MES or batch-execution, LIMS, EHS, CMMS or EAM, permit, inspection, incident, and regulatory-reporting records constrained by ISA, OSHA, EPA, and ISO public baselines.
The lane stops before engineering changes, EHS signoff, permit interpretation, regulator-facing certification, and other authority-side decisions even when schedule or throughput pressure is high.
